mechanism_narrative: A falling or near-zero federal funds rate lowers the discount rate on equity cash flows and supports risk appetite, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if lower FEDFUNDS levels or 3-month FEDFUNDS declines are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns no more often than the unconditional base rate across walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp deterioration in labor conditions reduces expected household income and earnings growth, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days after the unemployment signal breaches stress levels.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE crosses above 8.0% or rises more than 0.3pp over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if triggered labor-stress observations have mean 5-day S&P 500 returns greater than or equal to the unconditional mean, or if hit rate for negative returns is below 50%.

mechanism_narrative: A collapse in housing starts signals weak credit-sensitive demand and declining construction-linked profits, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if HOUST-below-600 episodes are followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of walk-forward triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Positive industrial production momentum signals improving factory utilization and operating leverage, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if 3-month INDPRO increases do not produce higher average 20-day S&P 500 returns than 3-month INDPRO decreases in rolling out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales growth exceeding consumer price growth signals real demand expansion rather than nominal inflation, raising expected revenues and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3-month growth crosses above CPIAUCSL 3-month growth
failure_condition: Reject if the RSAFS-over-CPIAUCSL growth spread has nonpositive Rank IC with 20-day S&P 500 returns over two consecutive 3-year walk-forward windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the policy rate is pinned near zero while unemployment remains very high, investors price continued policy support and liquidity, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if zero-rate high-unemployment episodes have median 60-day S&P 500 returns below zero or underperform all other regimes by more than 1 percentage point.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price acceleration combined with a rising federal funds rate tightens real financial conditions and compresses valuation multiples, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 6-month annualized growth crosses above 4% while FEDFUNDS rises over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if triggered inflation-tightening observations are followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of cases or show no underperformance versus baseline.

mechanism_narrative: Negative real GDP growth signals broad demand contraction and weaker aggregate earnings, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if GDPC1 year-over-year contractions are followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of triggered walk-forward observations.
